codeReview.summary: The commit substantially improves copy/layout structure (StepHeader + StepBody composition, Static-based status/copy, fingerprint and display-name rendering, and new Pilot coverage for wrapping/resize cases), but it leaves a blocking regression in username back-navigation state preservation after auto-setup prefill.

issues.description: _on_auto_setup_fail sets #username-status text but does not update _username_status_snapshot. Later, on_discovery_back restores #username-status from the stale snapshot (line 650), which clears the auto-detected status after a discovery round-trip. This breaks the existing back-nav invariant that username status is preserved.

VAL-VIS-026 calls for asserting no ANSI escapes in captured non-TTY output, but the added coverage only checks SetupScreen.render_sample_payload() for '\u001b[' and does not exercise captured setup output streams.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Touching copy/wrapping in setup.py can still regress pre-existing navigation invariants (auto-detected username status preservation) even when fulfills are purely visual; this cross-feature coupling is easy to miss during focused VAL-VIS work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature commit a86a042 updates _on_auto_setup_fail (setup.py:499-506) and retains status restoration via _username_status_snapshot (setup.py:650), but no new test in this feature covers the auto-setup prefill + discovery-back path.

summary: Reviewed feature copy-hierarchy-and-text-wrapping via handoff, transcript skeleton, skill file, and commit a86a042 diff. Result is fail due to a blocking regression in preserved username status after auto-setup prefill/back-nav; one additional non-blocking coverage gap was noted for non-TTY ANSI validation.
